title: Neon Reckoning: Issue #1
author: R. Vance
description: In a world of metal and madness, one hero rises to reclaim the grid. Experience the dawn of a new legend.

reviews.comment: The art kicks off swinging — absolutely electric opener.
Best #1 I've read this year. Sold on the whole arc.
Pacing is wild. Lost me for a panel or two but worth it.

title: Cyber Saints
author: K. Mori
description: When the city's AI gods turn on their makers, a band of outcasts becomes humanity's last prayer.

reviews.comment: Mori's worldbuilding is unreal. Every page drips atmosphere.
Great cast, a touch heavy on exposition early on.

title: The Last Warrior
author: H. Miller
description: A lone knight marches across a dying kingdom to face the prophecy that claimed everyone he loved.

reviews.comment: Heartbreaking and gorgeous. A modern classic.
The final pages had me speechless. Perfect.
Miller never misses. Buy it twice.

title: Cobalt Mystery
author: S. Lane
description: A rain-soaked detective chases a killer who only strikes under neon blue light.

reviews.comment: Pure noir mood. The blue palette is a character of its own.
Twist landed hard. Wish it ran a few pages longer.

title: Tree of Aeons
author: M. Chen
description: A wandering seed grows into a world-tree, and with it, the fate of every realm it touches.

reviews.comment: Quietly epic. The scale creeps up on you beautifully.

title: Ghost Protocol
author: J. Doe
description: A deleted agent comes back online with one directive: erase the people who erased her.

reviews.comment: Tense and stylish. Lead is ice-cold in the best way.
Solid revenge thriller, a bit familiar in spots.

title: Iron Vendetta
author: D. Castle
description: Framed and forgotten, a war hero builds a one-man army to bring down the syndicate that buried him.

reviews.comment: Brutal, lean, satisfying. Action choreography is top-tier.
Castle delivers. Villain could've used more depth.

title: Midnight Ledger
author: A. Frost
description: Every name in the ledger is a debt — and every debt is paid in blood by dawn.

reviews.comment: Creeping dread done right. Couldn't put it down.
Atmospheric and mean. Ending leaves you wanting more.
